signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has mild constructive tone, but not enough edge to justify fresh risk under the event brake.
SOL is constructive but too average to justify action while veto is active.
HEMI has some movement, but the edge is not clean enough to justify a fresh trade.
VVV has some improving squeeze-long traits, but the event brake keeps it in watch mode rather than buy mode.
BLAST is the strongest long on raw mechanics because funding is still negative and the squeeze profile is intact, but veto should keep it watch-only for now.
MET is too average on both edge and quality to justify action here.
AERO is a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the upside still looks fragile.
DASH remains a decent watch-only long, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
